lesson.subtitle: Aula 14 -- At the Restaurant
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Marlene Landucci | Aula 14 -- At the Restaurant

listenings.text: Good evening, and welcome to our restaurant. Let me tell you about the menu tonight. For the main dish, we have fresh fish with rice, or chicken with vegetables. Both come with a small salad. To drink, we have water, juice, and coffee. The water is free, but the juice is four dollars. For dessert, we have chocolate cake and fruit. The cake is very popular tonight. When you are ready, just call the waiter and we will take your order. At the end, we will bring you the bill. A tip is not necessary, but it is always welcome. Please enjoy your dinner with us.

Hi Marlene, this is Sofia. I made a reservation at the new restaurant near the square for tonight at eight. The menu looks wonderful. I will order the fish dish, and I think you will love the chicken. They have a nice drink menu too. I do not want any dessert, but you can order some cake if you like. Remember, we share the bill at the end and we leave a small tip for the waiter. The food there is not expensive. Come hungry. Call me when you are on your way. See you tonight.

extra_audio.text: First, you arrive at the restaurant and the waiter brings you the menu. Then you read the menu and choose a main dish. After that, you call the waiter and say, could I have the fish, please. Next, you order a drink and maybe some dessert. Finally, you ask for the bill, pay, and leave a small tip.
